##### About me

La Kiesha Davis, Student Midwife, Birth Doula, Certified Midwife Assistant, and Wellness Practitioner. My holistic journey began professionally 10 years ago and has grown into a complete lifestyle and passion. As a birth worker I have attended 106 births in various settings including home, birth center, and hospitals. Throughout my own life’s journey starting at a very young three years old, I have both fallen deeply in love with all things holistic and gathered various skills that have made me the woman I am today. My intention is to teach women to know and understand their worth and individuality by providing them hope and confidence in themselves. By integrating my knowledge of both western medicine as well as complementary and alternative medicine I am able to initiate self-efficacy for women throughout their lifespan and birth experiences. In my free time, you’ll find me at the beach, hiking, and spending time with my family and friends and always with a cup of tea.

##### Education

Bachelors in Complementary & Alternative Health (2014)

##### Service introduction

As a birth worker I am intuitive, gentle, and naturally in tune with the human body and nature. I am able to speak directly to the heart of a condition to initiate healing both medically and holistically. I am a teacher of female harmony, mind, body, and spirit. I cultivate the desire to be healthy and in tune with oneself.

##### Packages

Full Doula Service

Role of a doula
It is important for you to understand that I cannot - and do not - guarantee a specific outcome for your birth. I will make every effort to provide the services described here and agreed upon. This includes my presence at your birth, sometimes this is impossible (for example, precipitous labor and birth, preterm birth/induction). If I miss your birth due to your lack of calling me no funds will be returned. The investment is non-refundable.

On Call
In addition to our visits, I am available for phone and email consultations from the date that the agreement is signed. I encourage you to contact me with questions, concerns and any updates. I will get back to you as soon as I can – this will usually be immediately, but definitely within 48 hours. Please update me with any new developments and concerns throughout the pregnancy.
I will be on call for you during 37-42 weeks as well as available by phone via text or call. If for any reason, I do not answer when you call, please leave a message and also text me and I will return your call within an hour.

In Labor
If you think you are in labor please contact me as soon as possible. We will be in continual contact once labor has begun. Once you’re in active labor or you need me, I will head to you. I typically make it to you within an hour barring any unforeseen circumstances (depending on distance and traffic, emergencies, etc.).

Hi, I’m Stacy Soria, a certified birth doula and the heart behind My Safe Place Birth Coach.\\
\\
I’m a mama of four boys each with their own unique birth story and I’ve walked the journey of both breastfeeding and exclusive pumping. Those personal experiences, along with my deep passion for birthwork, led me to support other women in one of the most powerful moments of their lives.\\
\\
As a doula, my mission is to help you feel safe, seen, and supported—whether you’re planning a hospital birth, a VBAC, a water birth, or something in between. I believe birth is not just about bringing a baby into the world, but about bringing you into your power as a mother.\\
\\
I serve families with love, advocacy, and grounded education, and I walk beside you through it all your fears, your choices, your voice.\\
\\
Your birth story matters. Your body knows what to do. And I’m here to help you trust that every step of the way. 💛\\

Let’s build the birth experience you deserve.\\

Hi, I’m Daysy, a Doula, Fertility Life Coach, and Fertility Coordinator with over 13 years of experience supporting individuals and families through fertility, pregnancy, and loss. My background in reproductive medicine has allowed me to guide patients through complex journeys such as infertility, IVF, pregnancy, and postpartum care with both knowledge and compassion.\\
\\
My work is deeply rooted in personal experience. Having walked through miscarriage myself, I understand the emotional, physical, and mental toll these experiences can take. This perspective allows me to meet each client where they are — with empathy, patience, and without judgment.\\
\\
I believe that every question, concern, and emotion matters. Whether you are navigating a major life transition or seeking support through what may feel like a small moment, you deserve to feel heard and supported. My approach is centered on creating a safe, nurturing space where you feel empowered, informed, and cared for throughout your journey.\\

It is my passion to walk alongside you with compassion, advocacy, and unwavering support — honoring your story and helping you feel confident every step of the way.](https://www.bornbir.com/daysy-haith)

If you’re looking for a doula who is knowledgeable, present, and deeply committed to your experience; you’ve found her.\\

[![Yolanda Amezcua](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/miwv9ewk51yitf6qun3cg5by14of.png)\\

**Yolanda Amezcua**\\

10 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Yoli 🤍👶\\
A certified doula and experienced Labor & Delivery nurse, and co-founder of Vida Birth Services alongside my business partner, Mercedez.\\
\\
We are proud to be a Latina-owned business based in Riverside, California, serving families throughout the Inland Empire and all surrounding areas. As bilingual providers, we offer support in both English and Spanish to ensure every woman feels understood, respected, and truly cared for—without language barriers.\\
\\
With years of hands-on experience in labor, delivery, and emergency care, I’ve witnessed both the beauty of birth and the gaps that still exist in how women are prepared and supported. We’ve seen too many women walk into birth feeling overwhelmed, unheard, or unsure of their options—and we knew something needed to change.\\
\\
That’s why we created Vida Birth Services 💗\\
\\
Our mission is to empower women through education, advocacy, and personalized support—so they can walk into birth feeling confident, informed, and in control of their experience.\\
\\
At Vida, we offer comprehensive birth education classes designed to teach you exactly what’s happening in your body, what to expect during labor, your options for pain management, and how to recognize and respond to potential complications. We believe that education is power—and when a woman understands her body, she can advocate for herself and make the best decisions for her and her baby.\\
\\
In addition to education, we provide doula support that focuses on emotional reassurance, physical comfort, and strong advocacy during one of the most important moments of your life. From pregnancy through birth, you are never alone—we are there to guide, support, and stand beside you every step of the way.\\
\\
As Latina women in healthcare, this work is deeply personal to us. We are passionate about creating a safe space where women—especially women of color—feel seen, heard, and protected throughout their journey.\\
\\
Every mama deserves to feel heard.\\
Every birth deserves to be respected.\\
And every woman deserves to walk into that room feeling prepared, powerful, and supported.\\
\\
✨ Vida is more than a service—it’s a movement to change the birth experience.](https://www.bornbir.com/yolanda-amezcua)

[![Lori Kuettle](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/83eglmptb5fjmjoqwqun01ergo4c.jpeg)\\

**Lori Kuettle**\\
\\
45 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Accepts insurances\\
\\
\\
As your doula you will receive warm and personal care for your needs as you transition through your pregnancy. The focus will be about nutrition, exercise, self care, lactation, and plans for a safe and healthy post pregnancy recovery. As your postpartum doula, meal preparation, light housework, shopping, and caring for your baby will be provided to enable you to rest and recover.\\
I am a retired RN for L&D/pediatric newborns. I have 8 adult children, 16 grandchildren, and two dogs. In my spare time, I enjoy gardening, singing in my church choir, and traveling with family.\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
Having Lori as my doula completely changed my labor experience. I had 2 kids prior to finding Lori and she took care of me in every way possible. She made sure to understand my wants and needs before the big day came. She taught me some breathing techniques and how to relax the body. She was very detailed in her lessons, extremely sweet and caring. She treated my family as her own. When the big day came, Lori made sure that not only I was taken care of but my husband as well. I describe my labor experience as a day at the spa. We had music, dimmed lights, massages with essential oils and snacks. She made sure I was comfortable the whole time as well as my husband. She allowed my husband and I to experience the beauty of bringing a child into this world without all the stress of making sure my decisions with my labor was being honored. ## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds in Fontana, CA?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### How much does a doula cost in Fontana, CA?

The cost of a doula in Fontana, CA can vary depending on the doula's experience, training, and the services they provide. On average, the cost of a doula in Fontana, CA ranges from $800 to $2000. It's important to research and interview different doulas to find one that fits your budget and meets your needs.


### Are doulas covered by insurance in Fontana, CA?

Whether insurance covers the cost of a doula in Fontana, CA, depends on your specific insurance plan and provider. Some insurance companies may cover part or all of the cost if the doula's services are deemed medically necessary or included in a maternity care package. It's important to check with your insurance provider directly to determine coverage, as policies vary widely. In some cases, you may need to pay upfront and seek reimbursement. Additionally, California’s Medi-Cal program has started to expand coverage for doula services, so if you qualify for Medi-Cal, it may cover some doula-related costs. Always verify your benefits with your insurer or Medi-Cal representative for the most accurate information.


### What types of doulas are for hire in Fontana, CA?

In Fontana, CA, you can find various types of doulas available for hire, including birth doulas, postpartum doulas, and sometimes specialized doulas like antepartum doulas or bereavement doulas. Birth doulas provide physical, emotional, and informational support during labor and delivery, helping families navigate the birthing process. Postpartum doulas assist families after the baby’s arrival by offering support with newborn care, breastfeeding guidance, and household tasks to ease the transition. Antepartum doulas support individuals with high-risk pregnancies or those on bed rest, while bereavement doulas provide comfort and guidance to families experiencing pregnancy or infant loss. Availability and specialties may vary, so it’s best to research local doulas to find one that fits your specific needs.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.


### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of doulas what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).

##### About me

Amanda G
Doula Services
Birth and Postpartum Services
Placenta Encapsulation
Prenatal and Parent&Me Yoga Instructor

I am so honored to do this work. I am here to guide you through the journey of pregnancy, birth, and motherhood. Birth is a sacred ceremony. Whether you choose to birth in a hospital, birth center or at home, each is beautiful and deserving of celebration. As your doula, I believe in your vision, I trust in your strength, and I remind you of the power within you that has been passed down from generation to generation.

##### Education

Bachelor's of Science (2011)

##### Certification and training

Birth and Postpartum Doula Training (2017)

##### Specialties

BabywearingBelly BindingBirth DoulaBreastfeeding SupportChildbirth EducationFreebirthHome BirthHypnobirthing MethodOvernight Newborn CarePlacenta EncapsulationPostpartum Doula CarePregnancy, Prenatal, Postpartum Therapy

Show less


Show the rest


##### Languages

English

##### Accepted insurances

Inland Empire Health PlanKaiser Permanente

Show less


Show the rest


##### Service introduction

Hi! My name is Amanda McNair-Robinson and I've been a doula since 2017. I've attended over 100 births. I have also assisted in hospitals, birth centers and at home. I have helped many clients have successful VBACs.

KAISER and IEHP offer FREE services for its members. The services include prenatal visits, birth support and postpartum visits. Please reach out if you'd like to know more! My email is amandagdoula@gmail.com.

##### Ratings and reviews

Be the first to write Amanda a review!


Write a review

[Website](https://amandagdoulaservices.wixsite.com/website)[Instagram](https://instagram.com/Amandagdoula)

##### About me

Hi, I’m Stacy Soria, a certified birth doula and the heart behind My Safe Place Birth Coach.

I’m a mama of four boys each with their own unique birth story and I’ve walked the journey of both breastfeeding and exclusive pumping. Those personal experiences, along with my deep passion for birthwork, led me to support other women in one of the most powerful moments of their lives.

As a doula, my mission is to help you feel safe, seen, and supported—whether you’re planning a hospital birth, a VBAC, a water birth, or something in between. I believe birth is not just about bringing a baby into the world, but about bringing you into your power as a mother.

I serve families with love, advocacy, and grounded education, and I walk beside you through it all your fears, your choices, your voice.

Your birth story matters. Your body knows what to do. And I’m here to help you trust that every step of the way. 💛

Let’s build the birth experience you deserve.
@mysafeplacebirthcoach \| www.mysafeplacebirthcoach.com/website

##### Service introduction

My passion for birth work began in 2020 during the
pandemic when I was in labor with my second son. Due
to restrictions, I was only allowed one support person,
which meant I couldn’t have a doula by my side.
Fortunately, the nurse caring for me was also a doula.
The support she provided was transformative it made
me realize how impactful having someone by your side
can be during such a vulnerable time.
Since then, I’ve had the privilege of supporting
numerous families through their unique birth
experiences.
As your doula, my role is to provide continuous
emotional, physical, and informational support. I am
committed to respecting your birth preferences and
empowering you to make informed decisions. I
advocate for a birth experience where you feel safe,
heard, and fully supported.

##### Frequently asked questions

When should I call my doula during labor?

It’s best to contact your doula when you believe you are in active labor or if you have any concerns about early labor signs, even if you aren’t sure if you are actually in labor.


Can I hire a doula if I have a medical condition?

Yes! While I won’t be able to provide medical advice, I will still be there to walk you through the labor and delivery process and ensure that your needs and preferences are communicated to the medical team.


What happens if my doula is unavailable during labor?

I have a backup doula with professional training and experience who will be able to support you through labor and delivery if need be.


How do I prepare for postpartum?

We will discuss postpartum plans, including breastfeeding support, newborn care, and resources for emotional well-being during our prenatal visits.

The cost of a doula in Fontana, CA can vary depending on the doula's experience, training, and the services they provide. On average, the cost of a doula in Fontana, CA ranges from $800 to $2000. It's important to research and interview different doulas to find one that fits your budget and meets your needs.

The cost of a postpartum doula in Fontana, CA can vary depending on the specific services provided and the experience of the doula. On average, the cost can range from $25 to $50 per hour. Some doulas may also offer package deals or discounted rates for multiple sessions. It's best to reach out to individual doulas in the area to get specific pricing information.

It's important to note that doula fees can vary significantly within the Fontana area, with some highly experienced or specialized doulas charging higher rates. Additionally, some doulas offer package deals that may include prenatal visits, continuous labor support, and postpartum support, which can affect the overall cost.


When considering hiring a doula in Fontana, it's advisable to research and interview several doulas to find one who aligns with your preferences, needs, and budget. During consultations, you can discuss their specific services and pricing structure to ensure you have a clear understanding of what is included and the associated costs. This process will help you find a doula who meets your needs and fits within your budget.

Hi, I’m Yoli 🤍👶\\
A certified doula and experienced Labor & Delivery nurse, and co-founder of Vida Birth Services alongside my business partner, Mercedez.\\
\\
We are proud to be a Latina-owned business based in Riverside, California, serving families throughout the Inland Empire and all surrounding areas. As bilingual providers, we offer support in both English and Spanish to ensure every woman feels understood, respected, and truly cared for—without language barriers.\\
\\
With years of hands-on experience in labor, delivery, and emergency care, I’ve witnessed both the beauty of birth and the gaps that still exist in how women are prepared and supported. We’ve seen too many women walk into birth feeling overwhelmed, unheard, or unsure of their options—and we knew something needed to change.\\
\\
That’s why we created Vida Birth Services 💗\\
\\
Our mission is to empower women through education, advocacy, and personalized support—so they can walk into birth feeling confident, informed, and in control of their experience.\\
\\
At Vida, we offer comprehensive birth education classes designed to teach you exactly what’s happening in your body, what to expect during labor, your options for pain management, and how to recognize and respond to potential complications. We believe that education is power—and when a woman understands her body, she can advocate for herself and make the best decisions for her and her baby.\\
\\
In addition to education, we provide doula support that focuses on emotional reassurance, physical comfort, and strong advocacy during one of the most important moments of your life. From pregnancy through birth, you are never alone—we are there to guide, support, and stand beside you every step of the way.\\
\\
As Latina women in healthcare, this work is deeply personal to us. We are passionate about creating a safe space where women—especially women of color—feel seen, heard, and protected throughout their journey.\\
\\
Every mama deserves to feel heard.\\
Every birth deserves to be respected.\\
And every woman deserves to walk into that room feeling prepared, powerful, and supported.\\
\\
✨ Vida is more than a service—it’s a movement to change the birth experience.](https://www.bornbir.com/yolanda-amezcua)

Having Lori as my doula completely changed my labor experience. I had 2 kids prior to finding Lori and she took care of me in every way possible. She made sure to understand my wants and needs before the big day came. She taught me some breathing techniques and how to relax the body. She was very detailed in her lessons, extremely sweet and caring. She treated my family as her own. When the big day came, Lori made sure that not only I was taken care of but my husband as well. I describe my labor experience as a day at the spa. We had music, dimmed lights, massages with essential oils and snacks. She made sure I was comfortable the whole time as well as my husband. She allowed my husband and I to experience the beauty of bringing a child into this world without all the stress of making sure my decisions with my labor was being honored. She not only held the doctors and nurses accountable but she did the same to me with having my child naturally. She brought scripture to me that my husband would read to me which gave me the strength to get through it even when it was too hard and I wanted to give up. Even after the baby was here, lori made sure I was taken care of. She came to my home, allowed me to rest and helped with my daily routine so nothing fell through the cracks. She truly took care of me and my family. I will definitely use Lori again as my doula. \\
\\
\- Danielle M.](https://www.bornbir.com/lori-kuette)

\\
Hi sweet mama, welcome — and congratulations on your beautiful bundle of joy!\\
\\
My name is Selina, and I am a spirit-filled Christian doula and postpartum doula with over 2 years experience certified through DONA who is deeply passionate about serving and supporting mothers during one of the most important moments of their lives.\\
\\
Pregnancy and birth can bring so many emotions, questions, and unknowns, but you do not have to walk this journey alone. My heart is to help you feel calm, confident, empowered, and fully supported every step of the way—whether you gives birth at home or in the hospital, medicated or unmedicated. \\
I provide emotional, physical, and faith-based support with absolutely no judgment — just compassion, encouragement, and genuine care.\\
\\
I offer comfort measures, breathing techniques, visualization, labor support, spinning babies movements and continuous encouragement to help create a more peaceful and positive birth experience. My goal is for every mama I serve to feel heard, safe, educated, and cared for throughout her journey.\\
\\
During prenatal visits, I focus on education and preparation — I help you understand what to expect, clarify your preferences, and slow things down so you feel empowered and ready. When labor begins, I provide continuous emotional and physical support to help you feel grounded through each stage of the process. In the postpartum period, I meet you wherever you are — Sometimes that means feeding the baby while you pump, teaching you to pump, other times it’s just listening, making a snack, or simply admiring your baby with you.\\
Birth is just the beginning. I’m here to support your whole transition into parenthood — with warmth, wisdom, and deep respect for your journey.\\
\\
Whether this is your first baby or your fifth, you deserve to feel supported, empowered, and loved during your birth experience. I would be honored to walk alongside you during this special season.\\
\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
As a first time mama I had lots of questions and I means lots more then you could possibly imagine, god knew I needed someone like Selina . The night I was searching for my doula I came across many may doula pages and the moment I saw Selina’s profile my body filled with goosebumps I asked god to guide me to help pick what doula i should choose and there was Selina . Real story I’m not lying god helped me choose right . I didn’t grow up I can say with both parents in my life and even as an adult I don’t have my parents due to there own choices they made it there life . Selina was like my little mama angel that came by and guided me through the end of my pregnancy when things were getting really tough, through labor and delivery . I couldn’t ask for anyone better and I’m so grateful she was my doula . She answered my every question, listened to my cries and was supportive the whole way along . If you are having trouble choosing a doula this is your sign to search no further . Hello! My name is Annie. I am a DONA certified birth doula with a foundation in Spinning Babies techniques, proudly serving families throughout the SoCal area, with a deep passion for supporting women through one of the most transformative experiences of their lives. As a mother of four, I bring both personal and professional experience to my work, offering compassionate, nonjudgmental support throughout pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum period.\\
\\
My background as a personal trainer with a specialty in perinatal exercise, combined with my work as a health coach, allows me to support my clients holistically—physically, mentally, and emotionally. I believe that preparation and education are powerful tools, and I strive to help every woman feel informed, confident, and connected to her body throughout her journey.\\
\\
I am deeply committed to advocating for the women I serve. My goal is to help you feel seen, heard, and empowered as you make decisions that align with your values and vision for your birth. Whether you desire a calm, supported labor, guidance through the unknown, or reassurance in your strength, I am honored to walk alongside you.\\
\\
Every birth is unique, and it is my mission to help you approach yours feeling confident, focused, educated, and empowered.\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
Annie was my doula for the birth of my son in December 2024. This was my first baby so I was nervous about what birth would be like and if I could handle it since I wanted to go all natural. The hospital referred us to a list of doulas and after interviewing several, we met Annie. After we hung up the phone, my husband and I immediately said, "This is the one!" \\
Leading up to the delivery day we met with her to discuss our birth plans and what we hoped for. It was a very positive and helpful meeting. She brought up things I wouldn't have thought to consider.\\
When I went into labor, she and my husband were texting and she made her way quickly when we said we were ready for the extra support. She had different tools - tens unit, pressure holds, belly sling, diffuser/oils, and a lot more that I can't recall. Even more than the physical things she brought to support me, her presence was an incredible blessing. She is very good at adapting to the mood of the room. I wanted a quiet, dark environment and her calm, reassuring presence was exactly what I needed. She would suggest trying something new when she could tell I was starting to have a hard time getting through contractions, but would also give me space when I didn't want anyone near me or touching me. As I'd requested during our planning session, she and my husband worked together to support me throughout labor and they would quietly check in with each other about how I was doing and if they thought it was time to try something else. I wanted my husband to be my primary support person and Annie did a great job of coaching him and having a balance between supporting me and giving my husband and I space when I wanted it. They made a great team for me! Lastly, she has endurance! It was a long labor and she was with us for over 10 hours of it. She kept up her quiet, calm energy throughout and continued to support me physically too (like with holding my belly up in the sling or doing pressure squeezes on my forehead) for hours. That must have been tiring but she never gave any indication that it was. I am so grateful for her involvement in the birth of our son.\\
I truly could not have done it without the support of Annie - she was amazing! I absolutely want her there for the birth of our future babies and would recommend her to anyone! \\
\\
\- Madison G.](https://www.bornbir.com/anne-griffith)

I believe with every conviction that mothers hold innate wisdom to birth their babies. , I honor your intuition through holistic care and embodied practice—supporting you through pregnancy, birth, and the sacred postpartum transition.\\
\\
As your Sister in Christ, I offer faith-based birth support for those wanting so. We approach your labor as an act of worship—leaning on prayer and rejoicing to reveal God's strength and love for you.\\
\\
I am a community-first birthworker, passionate about making holistic wellness, coaching, and education accessible to women in my community.\\
\\
My work blends embodied childbirth education and hands-on support across pregnancy, birth, and postpartum. As a Birthing From Within–trained mentor, I offer education that goes far beyond information—our work honors the inner journey, emotional landscape, and transformation that birth invites.\\
\\
Alongside this, I provide holistic, nurturing care including herbal remedies, gentle bodywork, mindful movement, breathwork, and food as medicine—supporting the body’s natural physiology while tending to the nervous system and spirit.\\
\\
My Philosophy: Honoring the MotherBaby Dyad\\
\\
I intimately understand and uphold the crucial MotherBaby bond and believe in family-oriented care that follows natural instincts. This belief shapes every facet of my postpartum support and education:\\
\\
Attachment Parenting: I educate and support families in honoring bonding practices such as exclusive breastfeeding, safe bed-sharing/co-sleeping, elimination communication, and babywearing.\\
\\
Holistic Recovery: I offer nourishing, restorative care in the first 40 days, drawing from my training as a Postpartum Practitioner mentored by Pilar Chandler, Doctor of Ayurveda for Women’s Health.\\
\\
Lactation Philosophy: The Power of Human Milk\\
I am deeply committed to the evidence supporting human milk as the optimal standard for infant nutrition. While I support all feeding choices with heart, my philosophy is rooted in the conviction that exclusive breastfeeding provides unparalleled protection and nourishment. This is not just "good"; scientific consensus confirms that breast is best for both maternal and infant health outcomes.\\
\\

With years of experience as a midwife and birth professional, I support families through pregnancy, birth, and postpartum with a holistic, grounded approach that honors both physiology and personal choice.\\
Whether you are planning a home birth, hospital birth, birth center delivery, medicated or unmedicated experience, my role is to help you feel fully prepared — physically, emotionally, and mentally — for the birth you desire.\\
\\
My pregnancy bodywork sessions are designed to support optimal fetal positioning, pelvic balance, and nervous system regulation. When the body is aligned and tension patterns are released, babies can settle into ideal positions and labor often unfolds more smoothly. A regulated nervous system allows you to feel safer, more present, and more empowered — which can significantly impact both birth and postpartum recovery.\\
\\
In addition to hands-on bodywork, I offer comprehensive childbirth education that prepares you for the realities of labor, informed decision-making, partner support, and postpartum recovery. My goal is not to push a specific birth path, but to equip you with knowledge, confidence, and tools so you can navigate your experience feeling supported and in control.\\
\\
I bring both clinical insight and steady presence. I understand the nuances of hospital systems and home birth environments, and I help families advocate for themselves while staying connected to their intuition.\\
Birth is a rite of passage — and preparation matters. When your body is balanced, your nervous system is regulated, and your mind is informed, you enter birth grounded and ready.\\
It is an honor to walk alongside families as they prepare to welcome their babies and step into parenthood with confidence.](https://www.bornbir.com/ana-ruiz)

Hello! I’m Lindsay, a birth and postpartum doula and mom of 2.\\
\\
I became passionate about maternal wellness when I was expecting my first child in Norway. I received such quality care — midwives, visiting baby nurses, cushy parental leave! — but I was still missing the emotional support and expertise I needed to help me navigate a foreign medical system and the many anxieties I held as a first time parent.\\
\\
Since then, I’ve learned from hundreds of parents that they, too, needed more support than was available to them.\\
\\
Often times, they felt others were pushing an agenda that was not in their best interest. I became a doula to help change that, one family at a time.\\
\\
I’m here to hold space for you, learn with you, ease your fears, and empower you to feel confident through this important time in your life.\\
\\
If you are looking for a doula to help you birth and nurture your child on your own terms, please consider booking a free consultation with me. I’d love to learn more about you and see if we are a good fit.\\
\\
\\

October 10, 2025

ANGELINA, we have already made it clear to you. You have already went to yelp. We have reviewed your reviews., you go around making bad reviews because at a snap of a finger, one time visit we could not make due to an emergency induction for another mom. Out of 300 births, you are the only person who has ever tried to hurt my business the way you have. I personally feel that you have to heal in your postpartum that’s a priority to me versus my feelings at this point I have parted ways with you for more than one reason this is a public platform and I will not disclose any of your personal information. I did an absolutely fantastic job as doula , I did not ask you to write a review on this website, you made flaws claims and accusations about contracts. I have never made anyone sign. You made false claims about me not being communicative with you about a postpartum visit. You made false claims in regards to my professional professionalism. I helped watch your sweet baby boy while you were able to have a successful delivery. I have treated your family with absolute respect. But one thing I will not tolerate about my business as false accusation.. I will not move forward with any type of care in the future, and we have parted ways.

\- Justina B.

3.0

October 10, 2025

I was asked to do this review a bit early, which was off putting to me since services were not completed yet. However I will leave my honest review for services provided up til this point in time. Justina is a very sweet, personable, knowledgeable doula. She is available 24/7 by phone/text, however her in person availability is very limited. I find this to be a big issue especially when I stressed my area I would need most help is postpartum and I am now almost 4 weeks postpartum and still have yet to have a visit. Even prior to birth she did want to do one prenatal visit with my OBGYN, however that was rescheduled twice due to her availability and the next appt I never even made it to since my water broke early. I had my baby early, she was due October 18 but born Sep 14. Baby didn't get to leave nicu to come home until Sep 23 so it's been about 2 weeks of her being home and still no postpartum visits or scheduling of them without me initiating the conversation. I do know Justina had a vacation which I believe ended Sep 24 so I knew she wasn't available until after that, but getting a text from her asking for a review not anything about scheduling postpartum visits was very unsettling. I actually had even reached out to her Tuesday Sep 30 asking if we could schedule visits and she said she wasn't available til Monday to discuss so I said no worries. Then Friday she sent this yelp review text so imagine my surprise how a review took precedent over scheduling postpartum visits. We did schedule a visit for Wednesday 11am which was rescheduled on Wednesday morning to Friday. I fear that she is over booked and not every client gets the same attention as others due to this. This is not my first rodeo with a doula, however it is my first experience with such a popular one that doesn't have time to book my appts. She mentioned she has 7 moms in October, I was also due in October originally, so if I'd like my 1 hr postpartum visits scheduled I will need to reach out to her so she can pencil me in. I find this very unprofessional. I feel as if I'm booking a baby sitter at that point. My previous experience my doulas were reaching out to me to schedule postpartum visits, not the other way around. I booked and signed intake form with her back in July so to me scheduling should not be an issue with so much notice. Overall I feel she is a great birth doula, although I can't fully speak on that as my birth was C-section, but postpartum not so much. Again I have not yet had a postpartum visit but I stressed this was the most important to me as I don't have a village to help me, if I would've known it would be nearly impossible to get some of her time for those I would've booked someone else.

\- Angelina C.

##### About me

At Auratohn, we are dedicated to providing compassionate and culturally sensitive Doula support to women of color and single mothers during the transformative journey of childbirth. We believe that every woman deserves access to high-quality, evidence-based care that honors her unique needs and preferences. Our mission is to empower and advocate for these underrepresented communities by creating a safe, welcoming, and inclusive space where they can feel seen, heard, and supported. Through our skilled and personalized services, we strive to promote positive birth experiences, healthy outcomes, and lifelong connections between mothers, babies, and families. We are committed to breaking down barriers, challenging systemic inequalities, and promoting equity in maternal health care.

##### Certification and training

Birth and Postpartum Certification - Get Rooted Doula Training (2021)

##### Specialties

##### Service introduction

Congratulations on your journey to parenthood! As a dedicated and compassionate doula, I am honored to be a part of this transformative experience and offer my support to you and your growing family.

I firmly believe that everyone deserves access to doula care, regardless of their financial circumstances. Therefore, I am committed to working on a sliding scale for families in need. I understand that every situation is unique, and I am open to discussing flexible payment options that accommodate your specific financial situation. My goal is to ensure that every family can benefit from the support of a doula, regardless of their ability to pay the standard fee.

I would be delighted to further discuss my services, answer any questions you may have, and explore how we can work together to create a personalized doula support plan that meets your needs. Please feel free to reach out to me at your convenience to arrange a consultation.

Hi, I’m Susy, founder of Baby N Doula Care. I’m a certified postpartum doula and lactation educator, with specialized training in newborn care and NICU support. With over 18 years of experience, I provide thoughtful, evidence-based care to help families feel confident, supported, and well-rested during the early weeks at home. My approach is calm, respectful, and tailored to each family’s unique needs, offering both practical guidance and emotional support throughout the postpartum journey. \\

She goes above and beyond to make a meaningful impact through her doula services, bringing both compassion and professionalism to her work. It’s been a pleasure working with Susy and her team, and I highly recommend her to anyone looking for thoughtful, supportive care.\\
\\
\- Susan Lam](https://www.bornbir.com/susy-caracas)

[![Diana Roth](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/k19heoe3u0uua2p6p213e0nbqc0s.jpeg)\\

\\
A calling that has been nurtured for 32 years.\\
I began my career with children as a teenage babysitter. I have been fascinated by child development since I was little more than a child myself.\\
Arriving in the USA in 1991 from Barranquilla, Colombia I was immediately employed by Universal Studios Child Care Center. This experience offered a wide range of infants to care for and deepened my desire to\\
advance in my calling as a childcare specialist. It was in my interactions with various parents that I soon realized how what came naturally to me was frightening to some. In a very short time, parents were asking me to provide services outside of the care center. I was helping with establishing baby sleep schedules, nighttime care and creating baby routines for families.\\
In 1998 I had met my birthing doula trainer and mentor Carmen of Gyntle Touch Birth. At Carmen’s recommendation I also became a certified massage therapist from Touch Therapy Institute. Immediately I offered my time as a birthing and prenatal doula to help low-income unwed mothers. This was my way of giving back in gratitude of my being able to pursue my life’s purpose and obtain a vast amount of experience with a diverse group of mothers.\\
Over the last 32 years I have worked with hundreds of families, from birthing babies, preparing hospital care routines and directives, setting up nursery supplies, prenatal doula, lactation coach, night care, postpartum care, pregnancy and baby massage.\\
Along the way I married and was blessed to deliver my own two children, with my mentor Carmen presiding as my birthing doula.\\
A not so well-kept secret is that my husband and I are gourmet chefs, an additional service many families are\\
excited to have made available to them.\\
I consider myself truly blessed in that I am able to share in the most important part of a family. \\
Incredibly, many of the children I have had a hand in raising continue to contact me throughout their life. I have had the wonderfully good fortune to attend many, confirmations, bar & bat mitzvahs, graduations from elementary, Jr. High, High School, College and recently my first Graduate school ceremony.\\
I respect privacy and discretion, am able to travel, provide meal preparation and offer household management services.\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
We found Diana through Doulas of OC. She served as our birth doula as well as a postpartum night doula... If we have a second baby, Diana will be on speed dial again. If you are able to use Diana you will not be disappointed.\\
\\
\- Ariana](https://www.bornbir.com/diana-roth)

[![Nikki Avila](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/rtr31svt84raqk7kgb5ylzu0r3h0.jpeg)\\
\\
Bakersfield, California\\

**Nikki Avila**\\

9 years of experience\\

\\
\\
Hi, I’m Nikki Avila—Certified Birth Doula, Childbirth Educator, and Cofounder of Three Moons Doula Collective.\\
\\
My work is rooted in the belief that birth is sacred, transformative, and deeply personal. As a Latina birth worker, I’ve spent the last 16 years walking alongside families during one of life’s most vulnerable and powerful seasons—offering care grounded in compassion, cultural wisdom, and advocacy.\\
\\
Three Moons Doula Collective is not just a training program—it’s a movement. A space where doulas of color rise in their power, find their voice, and reclaim the knowledge that lives in their lineage. Our mission is to ensure that Black, Indigenous, and Latino families have access to skilled, culturally conscious birth support—because our communities deserve nothing less.\\
\\
Birth work has always lived in my spirit. Even before I had the language for it, I was holding space for pregnant friends as a teenager—offering a supportive hand to hold and an open heart to listen. That calling was modeled by my mother, who was a constant presence of strength and care in our family and community. I learned early on that showing up for others is powerful—and that’s where my passion was born.\\
\\
In 2018, I began formal training with Doula Trainings International and went on to earn certifications through Cornerstone Doula Training in birth and postpartum care. In 2021, I became a Certified Hypnobabies Instructor and Hypno-Doula, offering tools for calm, confident birthing experiences rooted in the power of the mind-body connection.\\
\\
My continued education reflects a deep commitment to both evidence-based care and ancestral wisdom. In 2022, I began training with H.E.R.B.A.L. Doula. In 2023, I trained with La Mujer de Tierra in Ceremony of Birth, deepening my connection to traditional and ceremonial practices. In 2024, I trained in Spinning Babies® with Midwife Tema, expanding my knowledge of physiological birth and body-based support. In 2025, I continued my holistic path by training in Reiki for birth with Mitra, integrating energetic support into my care.\\
\\
From belly binding to energy work, my approach is holistic and intentional—honoring the entire person, not just the process.\\
\\
In 2019, I founded the Bakersfield Doula Circle to unite birth workers in sisterhood and solidarity through monthly gatherings where doulas come together to share, learn, and lead with collective wisdom.\\
\\
I actively collaborate with community partners like Save a Mom, Save a Family, Inc., and since 2024, I have been part of the Maternal Healthcare Committee in Kern County, contributing to local efforts that aim to improve maternal health outcomes and expand access to culturally responsive care.\\
\\
In May 2024, I traveled to Ecuador with Wombs of the World, where I had the honor of sitting under and learning from Parteras Mama Mila and Partera Martha, a powerful mother-daughter duo. Their teachings deepened my understanding of birth as sacred lineage, passed down through generations, and strengthened my commitment to honoring traditional wisdom in my work.\\
\\
In February 2026, I continued this journey in Mexico City and Oaxaca, Mexico, where I sat with and witnessed the work of traditional midwives and healers—Doña Queta, Partera Paula, Partera Yuu, Curandera Norma, Partera Rosa of Valenzuela, and Partera Mariela. These experiences further shaped my understanding of birth as ceremony, community, and ancestral remembrance. I am deeply grateful for the work and mission of Wombs of the World and for Charlotte’s leadership in creating these transformative spaces.\\
\\
In 2025, through Three Moons Doula Collective, we trained our first cohort of doulas—many of whom are now actively working with managed care plans, providing essential support to mothers throughout Kern County.\\
\\
As of 2026, my colleagues and I continue to expand this work by training Native American Indigenous birth workers, strengthening culturally rooted care within our communities.\\
\\
As a Certified VBAC Link Educator and Medi-Cal Doula, I’ve supported over 130 births, including more than 28 Medi-Cal births in 2024 alone. Each one is a powerful reminder of how informed, respectful care can transform outcomes and empower families.\\
\\
In June 2026, I will be releasing The Wisdom of Waiting Birth Method™, a childbirth education course designed to guide families back to trust, knowledge, and the innate power of birth.\\
\\
Through Three Moons Doula Collective, I’m committed to shifting the paradigm. This work is about more than teaching skills—it’s about nurturing leaders. Doulas who are deeply rooted in their culture, confident in their knowledge, and ready to create generational change.\\
\\
I hope every person who trains with us feels seen, held, and empowered—and that every birthing person they support feels the same.\\
\\
This is the legacy I’m building: one of advocacy, ancestral remembering, and powerful community care. We are creating a future where birth is safer, more just, and deeply sacred.\\
\\
I am here to support you, IN THE WAITING,\\
\\
Nikki Avila\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
The one thing I regret is not hiring Nikki earlier!! I reached out to her when I was 36 weeks pregnant and I am so lucky she was willing to take me on so late in my pregnancy.\\
\\
Nikki not only helped me prepare as best as I could in the short time frame we had before I gave birth, but she also connected me with the women in her team who were incredible resources as well.\\
\\
When it came to my L&D, Nikki was with me in the hospital for a complete 24 hours. She helped me navigate challenges to my birth preferences, consider tough decisions, and advocate for me when dealing with some difficult healthcare professionals. I truthfully think my birth experience would have taken a horrible turn if i did not have the support I received from Nikki.\\
\\
After I was released from the hospital, My son had difficulty with his latch, and i had a hard time with pumping. I shared my issues with Nikki, and she quickly put me in touch with her lactation consultant in her group, and they were able to counsel me that same day. I truthfully believe if it wasn't for this help, i would have given up on my breast feeding journey prematurely.\\
\\
Although I reached out to Nikki late only because i found out my insurance would cover a doula, My husband and I both have determined if we no longer could hire Nikki through our insurance, we would budget for her services if we decide to have another baby in the near future.\\
\\
Nikki is kind but also direct, gentle but also strong, informative but not condescending, and she is patient but not avoidant. She is sensitive to the needs of the individual client, and accepting to all walks of life.\\
\\
\- Aliyah Gonzalez](https://www.bornbir.com/nikki-avila)

## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### Are doulas covered by insurance?

In the US, doula coverage really depends on your plan and where you live—Medicaid in about 18 states (plus DC) now covers birth doulas, a handful of states require private insurers to include them, but most private plans and Medicare don't, and CHIP mirrors your state’s Medicaid rules. In Canada, there's no public coverage, so you'll pay out‑of‑pocket unless your employer's extended health or spending‑account plan reimburses certified doula fees, and there's no CRA tax credit for it. Many doulas on Bornbir offer payment plans or sliding scale fees to make their services more accessible to a wider range of families.


### How much does a doula cost if I'm paying out of pocket?

The cost of hiring a doula can vary significantly based on factors such as location, experience, and the range of services offered. On average, doula services in the United States can range from $800 to $2,500 or more. This fee typically covers prenatal visits, continuous labor support, postpartum follow-up, and educational resources. In some cases, doulas may offer additional services such as childbirth education classes or lactation consulting, which can contribute to a higher fee.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.


### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of doulas what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).

# Find a Doula Near Me in Rialto, CA

Answer a few questions, and match directly with top-rated and certified doulas near you in Rialto, CA - all in 30 seconds, and it's entirely free!


Get matched now

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/sipqsp5rstgzzq7sgfhr.png)

### Save time

No more endless searching online


### Save money

Compare your options and pricing


### Global movement

Both in-person and virtual support available


## How to find a doula near me

01

### Tell us your doula needs

Answer a few quick questions regarding your due date, birth plan, location, budget etc.


02

### Match with doulas

We will search our database of 4,000+ doulas and instantly bring a list of certified doulas to you.


03

### Start messaging

Chat with your matched doulas, compare options and schedule consultations to find the right fit.


### Get matched

Share details about your doula needs ranging from birth prep, breastfeeding, baby sleep, postpartum recovery, or newborn care, and we will match you with the best local doulas.


Find your doula

![Match with providers](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto,w_490/production/static/agx03khe9ibcmq3fsuzz.png)

### Extensive doula network

Begin matching with our network of 4,000+ doulas, easily message those who match your needs, and show interest in learning more.


Match with a doula

![Discover providers near you](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto,w_490/production/static/fqndvrdg7pmnmjnrl4zo.png)

### Real parent reviews

Gain insight from reviews by fellow parents to make a well-informed decision.


Get started now

![Real parent reviews](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,w_490/production/static/znrhwspd8de0g9xcbwuq.png)

Found 330 Doulas near Rialto, CA


[![Jimena Calderon](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/mpb1q64jr6z8phw3fb6bw78tmhl7.png)\\
\\
Rialto, California\\
\\
**Jimena Calderon**\\
\\
Hi! My name is Jimena, and I am passionate about supporting families through pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum period. My philosophy as a doula is centered on advocacy, education, and compassionate support. I believe every person deserves to feel informed, respected, and empowered throughout their birth experience.\\
My goal is to help make every step of pregnancy and birth as comfortable, calm, and supported as possible. I strive to create a safe space where families feel heard, confident in their choices, and fully supported during one of the most meaningful moments of their lives.](https://www.bornbir.com/jimena-calderon)

[![Stacy Soria](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/e1rgnkx73d6p7ggvdtuldblbg26b.png)\\
\\
Fontana, California\\
\\
**Stacy Soria**\\
\\
7 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Accepts insurances\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Stacy Soria, a certified birth doula and the heart behind My Safe Place Birth Coach.\\
\\
I’m a mama of four boys each with their own unique birth story and I’ve walked the journey of both breastfeeding and exclusive pumping. Those personal experiences, along with my deep passion for birthwork, led me to support other women in one of the most powerful moments of their lives.\\
\\
As a doula, my mission is to help you feel safe, seen, and supported—whether you’re planning a hospital birth, a VBAC, a water birth, or something in between. I believe birth is not just about bringing a baby into the world, but about bringing you into your power as a mother.\\
\\
I serve families with love, advocacy, and grounded education, and I walk beside you through it all your fears, your choices, your voice.\\
\\
Your birth story matters. Your body knows what to do. And I’m here to help you trust that every step of the way. 💛\\
\\
Let’s build the birth experience you deserve.\\
@mysafeplacebirthcoach \| www.mysafeplacebirthcoach.com/website](https://www.bornbir.com/stacy-soria)

[![Nicole Jones](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/4g432mkolhuscekkdt0ucg99733s.jpeg)\\
\\
Grand Terrace, California\\
\\
5.0\\
\\
(29 reviews)\\
\\
\\
**Nicole Jones**\\
\\
2 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Accepts insurances\\
\\
\\
Hi, my name is Nicole, and I am a birth and postpartum doula dedicated to helping women and families experience birth in a way that feels empowered, supported, and aligned with their vision.\\
\\
I believe every mother deserves to feel heard, respected, and confidently guided throughout her journey. I provide personalized support that blends emotional, physical, and spiritual care, while also offering strong advocacy when needed; ensuring you feel safe, informed, and fully supported every step of the way.\\
\\
With over 25 years of experience as an HR Manager, I bring a unique layer of expertise to my work. I am well-versed in California laws and leave practices, allowing me to support not only your birth experience but also your transition into postpartum life with clarity and confidence.\\
\\
My approach is hands-on, intuitive, and grounded in both knowledge and real-life application. I meet you where you are, support your goals, and help create a calm, nurturing environment where you can truly thrive.\\
\\
If you’re looking for a doula who is knowledgeable, present, and deeply committed to your experience; you’ve found her.\\
\\
It would be an honor to support you on your journey into motherhood.\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
\\
\\
I want to share my personal thoughts about Doula Nicole. She was a doula for my daughter. As a mom you want only the best for your children, no matter how old they are. Doula Nicole was the "Best" for my daughter duringher entire pregnancy. She was experienced, professional, caring, and supportive. Nicole extended herself beyond measure to encourage and inspire my daughter as she went through all the emotional and physical challenges that comes with being pregnant. \\
\\
My prayer is that every woman deserves that opportunity to feel like she matters. Nicole has that gift to share with you. We as a family are grateful for her services. I know you will be too.\\
\\
Adrienne \\
\\
\- Adrienne C.](https://www.bornbir.com/nicole-jones)

[![Justina Buenrostro](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/ktz3gwnq9s1clcrq6n3orneq2axh.jpeg)\\
\\
Fontana, California\\
\\
4.0\\
\\
(2 reviews)\\
\\
\\
**Justina Buenrostro**\\
\\
18 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Accepts insurances\\
\\
\\
I am a mother to seven children , I understand every stage of a mama to be , it’s a honor to be a womens doula! You are my Queen before during & after baby’s arrival ! \\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
ANGELINA, we have already made it clear to you. You have already went to yelp. We have reviewed your reviews., you go around making bad reviews because at a snap of a finger, one time visit we could not make due to an emergency induction for another mom. Out of 300 births, you are the only person who has ever tried to hurt my business the way you have. I personally feel that you have to heal in your postpartum that’s a priority to me versus my feelings at this point I have parted ways with you for more than one reason this is a public platform and I will not disclose any of your personal information. I did an absolutely fantastic job as doula , I did not ask you to write a review on this website, you made flaws claims and accusations about contracts. I have never made anyone sign. You made false claims about me not being communicative with you about a postpartum visit. You made false claims in regards to my professional professionalism. I helped watch your sweet baby boy while you were able to have a successful delivery. I have treated your family with absolute respect. But one thing I will not tolerate about my business as false accusation.. I will not move forward with any type of care in the future, and we have parted ways.\\
\\
\- Justina B.](https://www.bornbir.com/justina-buenrostro)

[![Daysy Haith](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/deba0aazrkda9lloi81ofkwvgwbr.png)\\
\\
Riverside, California\\
\\
**Daysy Haith**\\
\\
Accepts insurances\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Daysy, a Doula, Fertility Life Coach, and Fertility Coordinator with over 13 years of experience supporting individuals and families through fertility, pregnancy, and loss. My background in reproductive medicine has allowed me to guide patients through complex journeys such as infertility, IVF, pregnancy, and postpartum care with both knowledge and compassion.\\
\\
My work is deeply rooted in personal experience. Having walked through miscarriage myself, I understand the emotional, physical, and mental toll these experiences can take. This perspective allows me to meet each client where they are — with empathy, patience, and without judgment.\\
\\
I believe that every question, concern, and emotion matters. Whether you are navigating a major life transition or seeking support through what may feel like a small moment, you deserve to feel heard and supported. My approach is centered on creating a safe, nurturing space where you feel empowered, informed, and cared for throughout your journey.\\
\\
It is my passion to walk alongside you with compassion, advocacy, and unwavering support — honoring your story and helping you feel confident every step of the way.](https://www.bornbir.com/daysy-haith)

[![Marlene Ariza](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/vxgjr5yrv0bog77fn7fzxvyi60sz.png)\\
\\
Riverside, California\\
\\
**Marlene Ariza**\\
\\
4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Accepts insurances\\
\\
\\
Wild Womb Birth Co. 🌕 brand story \\
\\
Birth work is more than a job to me. \\
It is a calling that lives in the body, instinct, & in the womb.\\
\\
Wild Womb Birth Co. was created from a deep devotion to women and the sacred work of birth. I believe birth is not something to be managed or controlled, but something to be held, honored, and trusted.\\
\\
The “wild” speaks to intuition - our innate knowing that exists beneath fear and outside noise. The “womb” represents creation, wisdom, and the space where transformation begins - not just for babies, but for mothers.\\
\\
My work is rooted in presence, advocacy, and reverence for each woman’s unique journey. I walk alongside women during one of the most vulnerable and powerful seasons of their lives, offering grounded support, education, and unwavering belief in their bodies.](https://www.bornbir.com/marlene-ariza)

[![Yolanda Amezcua](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/miwv9ewk51yitf6qun3cg5by14of.png)\\
\\
Riverside, California\\
\\
**Yolanda Amezcua**\\
\\
10 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Yoli 🤍👶\\
A certified doula and experienced Labor & Delivery nurse, and co-founder of Vida Birth Services alongside my business partner, Mercedez.\\
\\
We are proud to be a Latina-owned business based in Riverside, California, serving families throughout the Inland Empire and all surrounding areas. As bilingual providers, we offer support in both English and Spanish to ensure every woman feels understood, respected, and truly cared for—without language barriers.\\
\\
With years of hands-on experience in labor, delivery, and emergency care, I’ve witnessed both the beauty of birth and the gaps that still exist in how women are prepared and supported. We’ve seen too many women walk into birth feeling overwhelmed, unheard, or unsure of their options—and we knew something needed to change.\\
\\
That’s why we created Vida Birth Services 💗\\
\\
Our mission is to empower women through education, advocacy, and personalized support—so they can walk into birth feeling confident, informed, and in control of their experience.\\
\\
At Vida, we offer comprehensive birth education classes designed to teach you exactly what’s happening in your body, what to expect during labor, your options for pain management, and how to recognize and respond to potential complications. We believe that education is power—and when a woman understands her body, she can advocate for herself and make the best decisions for her and her baby.\\
\\
In addition to education, we provide doula support that focuses on emotional reassurance, physical comfort, and strong advocacy during one of the most important moments of your life. From pregnancy through birth, you are never alone—we are there to guide, support, and stand beside you every step of the way.\\
\\
As Latina women in healthcare, this work is deeply personal to us. We are passionate about creating a safe space where women—especially women of color—feel seen, heard, and protected throughout their journey.\\
\\
Every mama deserves to feel heard.\\
Every birth deserves to be respected.\\
And every woman deserves to walk into that room feeling prepared, powerful, and supported.\\

## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds in Rialto, CA?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### How much does a doula cost in Rialto, CA?

The cost of a doula in Rialto, CA can vary depending on the experience and services offered by the doula. On average, the cost of a doula in Rialto, CA can range from $800 to $2000 for prenatal, birth, and postpartum support. It is recommended to contact individual doulas in the area to inquire about their specific pricing and services.


### Are doulas covered by insurance in Rialto, CA?

Whether insurance will cover the cost of a doula in Rialto, CA, depends on your specific insurance plan and provider. Some insurance companies offer partial or full reimbursement for doula services, especially if they are considered medically necessary or contribute to better maternal and newborn outcomes. You should contact your insurance provider directly to inquire about coverage, as policies vary widely. Additionally, some Medicaid programs in California may cover doula services, so checking eligibility under Medi-Cal could be beneficial. If insurance does not cover it, you might also explore flexible spending accounts (FSA) or health savings accounts (HSA) to help with the cost.


### What types of doulas are for hire in Rialto, CA?

In Rialto, CA, you can typically find various types of doulas available for hire, including birth doulas, postpartum doulas, and sometimes specialized doulas such as antepartum doulas, bereavement doulas, and fertility doulas. Birth doulas provide emotional, physical, and informational support during labor and childbirth. Postpartum doulas assist families in the weeks following birth by helping with newborn care, emotional support, and household tasks. Antepartum doulas support individuals experiencing high-risk pregnancies or bed rest. Bereavement doulas offer care and guidance for those experiencing pregnancy or infant loss, and fertility doulas support individuals or couples navigating fertility challenges. Availability may vary, so it's recommended to research local doula services or agencies in Rialto for specific offerings.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.
